message:
feat(tmux): add context.Context support for cancellable operations

Introduce context-aware variants for all blocking tmux operations,
enabling proper cancellation and timeout handling for dashboard
refresh cycles and other concurrent workflows.

Changes:
- Client.RunContext() and runLocalContext() for context-aware command
  execution with proper context.Err() propagation on cancellation
- runSSHContext() for remote tmux operations with cancellation support
- Client.RunSilentContext() for fire-and-forget commands with context
- GetPanesContext(), GetPanesWithActivityContext() for pane enumeration
  with cancellation support
- CapturePaneOutputContext() for pane capture with timeout capability

Additional improvements:
- Change tmux format separators from "|#|" and ":" to "|===|" to avoid
  conflicts with session names containing colons or special characters
- Add chunked SendKeys() implementation (4KB chunks) to handle large
  payloads without hitting ARG_MAX or tmux buffer limits
- Strengthen ValidateSessionName() to reject "/" and "\" in addition
  to existing ":" and "." restrictions

The context-aware methods enable the dashboard to cancel in-flight
tmux operations when the user navigates away or requests a refresh,
preventing stale data from accumulating and reducing resource waste.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -80,9 +80,11 @@ func TestValidateSessionName(t *testing.T) {
 		{"my-project", false},
 		{"my_project", false},
 		{"MyProject123", false},
-		{"", true},           // empty
-		{"my.project", true}, // contains .
-		{"my:project", true}, // contains :
+		{"", true},            // empty
+		{"my.project", true},  // contains .
+		{"my:project", true},  // contains :
+		{"my/project", true},  // contains /
+		{"my\\project", true}, // contains \
 	}
 
 	for _, tt := range tests {
